title: Conceptual Overview of Compactness in Metric Spaces
description: A visual map showing the key definitions, properties, and logical connections that define a compact space and its relationship to other fundamental concepts like closed sets, boundedness, and continuity.
relationships: A set K is Compact if every Open Cover has a Finite Subcover
In Metric Spaces: Compact ⇔ Sequentially Compact ⇔ (Totally Bounded and Complete)
Heine-Borel: In ℝⁿ, Compact ⇔ Closed and Bounded
If f is Continuous and X is Compact, then f(X) is Compact
